skill.name: when-optimizing-prompts-use-prompt-architect
skill.description: Framework for analyzing, restructuring, testing, and refining prompts for AI systems using prompt engineering techniques.
skill.summary: Optimize AI prompts with structured analysis, examples, validation, and iterative refinement.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and high-risk pattern matches, but most are fenced markdown examples or prompt templates, not executable project code. The confirmed concern is that the skill recommends running external npx claude-flow commands and includes shell templates that pass prompt content through command-line arguments. No evidence found of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, network callbacks, or prompt injection attempts.

medium_findings.title: External Command Recommendations in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly recommends running npx claude-flow@alpha commands. This is a legitimate workflow pattern, but it depends on executing an external package and should require user approval in controlled environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and executable if copied. Confidence is not higher because they are examples inside markdown, not scripts that run automatically.

medium_findings.title: Shell Templates Pass Prompt Content Through CLI Arguments
medium_findings.description: The example scripts use command substitution and shell variables to pass prompt and test data into claude-flow tasks. This can mishandle untrusted prompt files, expose prompt content to command history or subprocesses, and create unsafe copy-paste patterns.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The unsafe pattern is visible in shell templates. Risk is moderate because the content is documentation and no automatic execution path was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak cryptography matches do not correspond to hash functions or encryption logic in the reviewed content. They appear to be caused by ordinary prompt-engineering text, markdown examples, or template placeholders.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations contain descriptive text or prompt structure examples, not cryptographic APIs. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, or similar weak algorithms being used.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Matches Are Prompt Examples
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance matches are contained in ReAct-style prompt examples and workflow text. No host enumeration, credential discovery, or local system probing behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe AI prompt evaluation or illustrative reasoning steps. They do not instruct the agent to inspect the host system or collect sensitive machine data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Executable Shell Snippets Using npx
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes shell snippets that run npx claude-flow@alpha. These should be treated as user-approved commands rather than silently executed by an agent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is explicit across the documentation. It is a workflow dependency, not evidence of malware.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Substitution in Example Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: The bash templates assign command output to variables with command substitution. This is common shell usage, but it can become unsafe when adapted with untrusted file paths or prompt content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The syntax is present, but it appears only in example templates. The risk depends on whether users copy and execute the snippets with untrusted inputs.

content.user_title: Optimize AI Prompts with Prompt Architect
content.value_statement: Teams often struggle with vague prompts, inconsistent AI outputs, and weak validation methods. This skill provides a structured process for analyzing, improving, testing, and documenting prompts.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes prompts for missing context, unclear tasks, weak constraints, and absent output formats.
Reorganizes prompts into system context, role, task, constraints, format, examples, and quality criteria.
Adds prompt engineering patterns such as few-shot examples, constraint framing, ReAct, and self-consistency.
Defines A/B test cases and metrics for comparing original and optimized prompts.
Produces an optimization report with changes, validation results, and reuse guidance.
limitations: It provides a framework and templates, not a guaranteed automatic quality improvement.
Validation depends on representative test cases and clear scoring criteria supplied by the user.
Some examples reference claude-flow commands that may not be available in every environment.
It does not replace domain review for legal, medical, financial, or safety-critical prompts.

use_cases.title: Improve Agent System Prompts
use_cases.description: Refine role instructions, constraints, output formats, and examples for specialized AI agents.

use_cases.title: Standardize Prompt Libraries
use_cases.description: Create reusable prompt entries with consistent structure, quality criteria, and validation notes.

use_cases.title: Test Prompt Changes Before Release
use_cases.description: Compare original and optimized prompts with test cases, score changes, and document results.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Prompt Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a prompt gives inconsistent or vague answers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this prompt for clarity, missing context, weak constraints, and output format gaps. Suggest a clearer version and explain each change.

prompt_templates.title: Structured Rewrite
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a prompt needs a full organization pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this prompt using sections for context, role, task, constraints, output format, examples, and quality criteria. Keep the user goal unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Validation Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when prompt changes need measurable comparison.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an A/B test plan for this original and revised prompt. Include test cases, scoring criteria, expected outputs, and failure signals.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Prompt Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for complex agent or workflow prompts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this prompt as a production AI instruction. Identify anti-patterns, add evidence-based techniques, define constraints, propose tests, and produce release notes.

output_examples.input: A short support prompt that says, "Answer customer questions quickly."
output_examples.output: The skill would add support context, agent role, allowed actions, escalation rules, tone requirements, and a concise answer format.

output_examples.input: An agent prompt with inconsistent formatting and no success criteria.
output: Identifies missing output format and unclear constraints.
Creates a structured prompt with examples.
Adds measurable quality criteria for review.

output_examples.input: Two prompt versions that need release validation.
output_examples.output: The skill would define test cases, compare outputs, score consistency, and summarize whether the optimized prompt is ready.

best_practices: Start with a clear task and audience before adding advanced prompting techniques.
Use realistic test cases that include normal, edge, and ambiguous inputs.
Document why each prompt change was made and how success will be measured.
anti_patterns: Do not add chain-of-thought instructions when concise final answers are required.
Do not optimize prompts without preserving the original business goal.
Do not trust benchmark improvements without reviewing the test cases and scoring method.

faq.question: What does this skill optimize?
faq.answer: It optimizes prompts, agent instructions, output formats, examples, constraints, and validation plans.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides testing templates and workflows. Actual execution depends on the tools available in your environment.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompt structure and review process are useful across Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and other LLM tools.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It has medium risk because it includes external command examples. It is not blocked, but command execution should require user approval.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Provide the current prompt, target task, desired output format, constraints, examples, and success criteria.

faq.question: What should reviewers check?
faq.answer: Review whether the optimized prompt preserves intent, handles edge cases, and avoids unsafe or unsupported instructions.
